questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what kind of object is the Kaustubha?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha is celebrated in the Puranas as the most radiant of jewels.

questions.q: Which deity wears the Kaustubha gem upon his chest?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Vishnu took the Kaustubha to adorn his chest.

questions.q: From which great event did the Kaustubha gem emerge?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha was one of the treasures that arose from the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: On which part of Vishnu's body is the Kaustubha jewel said to shine?
questions.explanation: In iconography the Kaustubha shines upon Vishnu's breast, beside the Srivatsa mark.

questions.q: The Sanskrit name of the churning of the ocean that produced the Kaustubha is known as what?
questions.explanation: The episode is called the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the cosmic ocean.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is described in the tradition as one of the precious treasures of which substance churned from the sea?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha emerged from the churning of the ocean of milk (Kshira Sagara).

questions.q: Vishnu is widely venerated in the Hindu tradition by which title that the Kaustubha serves as an emblem of?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha stands as a recognized emblem of Vishnu, who is venerated as the Preserver.

questions.q: In the churning that produced the Kaustubha, the two opposing groups who pulled the churning rope were the devas and which others?
questions.explanation: The devas and asuras together churned the ocean of milk in the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is best classified as which type of sacred item in Hindu lore?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha is a divine treasure, the jewel that adorns Vishnu.

questions.q: The texts that recount how Vishnu took up the Kaustubha gem belong chiefly to which body of literature?
questions.explanation: It is the Puranas that relate how Vishnu took the Kaustubha to adorn his chest.

questions.q: Alongside the Kaustubha gem, which curl-like mark is described on Vishnu's chest in iconography?
questions.explanation: In iconography the Kaustubha shines alongside the Srivatsa mark on Vishnu's breast.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which mountain served as the churning rod during the Samudra Manthana that yielded the Kaustubha?
questions.explanation: Mount Mandara was used as the churning rod in the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: Which serpent was used as the churning rope during the Samudra Manthana that produced the Kaustubha?
questions.explanation: The serpent Vasuki was wound about Mount Mandara to serve as the churning rope.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is counted among the treasures of the ocean churning, the same event that also produced which nectar of immortality?
questions.explanation: The Samudra Manthana produced both the Kaustubha and Amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: The goddess Lakshmi, consort of Vishnu who also arose from the ocean churning, is associated with the Kaustubha-bearing Vishnu in what role?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, who also emerged from the churning of the ocean, is the consort of Vishnu who wears the Kaustubha.

questions.q: In the Samudra Manthana, the avatar of Vishnu who supported the sinking Mount Mandara on his back was which form?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the form of Kurma, the tortoise, to bear Mount Mandara upon his back during the churning.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is one of the auspicious treasures of the churning; which physician-god rose from the same waters bearing the pot of nectar?
questions.explanation: Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods, rose from the churned ocean bearing the pot of Amrita.

questions.q: In Vaishnava iconography, the Kaustubha gem is most often depicted as set in what manner upon Vishnu?
options: As a ring upon his toe
As a band around his upper arm only
As a jewel worn on his chest or neck
As a clasp on his sandal
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha is shown as a brilliant jewel adorning Vishnu's chest or hung at his neck.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is the foremost of jewels; the white conch that Vishnu also bears is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Panchajanya is Vishnu's conch; though distinct from the Kaustubha gem, both are emblems of the Preserver.

questions.q: The radiant Kaustubha jewel is traditionally said to symbolize what aspect associated with Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha is celebrated for its dazzling radiance, symbolizing the splendor and purity of Vishnu.

questions.q: The Samudra Manthana that yielded the Kaustubha is most fully narrated in which body of texts and the epics?
questions.explanation: The churning of the ocean is narrated in the Puranas and in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: The Kaustubha emerged from the ocean of milk, which in Sanskrit cosmology is named what?
questions.explanation: The ocean of milk that was churned is called the Kshira Sagara in Sanskrit.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is counted among the treasures of the Samudra Manthana; which celestial wish-fulfilling cow is also named among those same treasures?
questions.explanation: The wish-fulfilling cow Kamadhenu is, like the Kaustubha, named in the Puranas among the treasures that arose from the churning of the ocean.

questions.q: In the Puranas, after the Kaustubha emerged from the churning, who claimed it to wear upon his person?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Vishnu himself took up the Kaustubha to adorn his chest.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is reckoned among the ratnas (jewels or treasures) of the churning; which goddess of fortune is likewise counted among those treasures?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, also called Shri, is among the celebrated treasures (ratnas) that arose from the churning.

questions.q: In the iconographic descriptions, the Kaustubha resting on Vishnu's chest is poetically said to mirror what quality of the deity?
options: His role as a wandering ascetic
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha's brilliance is taken to reflect the radiant, all-illuminating presence of Vishnu the Preserver.

questions.q: During the Samudra Manthana that produced the Kaustubha, the dreadful poison that first arose was consumed by which deity?
questions.explanation: Shiva drank the Halahala poison that emerged before the treasures, including the Kaustubha, appeared.

questions.q: The Kaustubha is grouped with Vishnu's distinguishing marks; which of these is the lock of hair or curl-mark, NOT a separate gem, that accompanies it on his chest?
questions.explanation: The Srivatsa is the auspicious curl-mark on Vishnu's chest that accompanies the Kaustubha gem.

questions.q: In Vaishnava tradition the Kaustubha, as the jewel of Vishnu, is sometimes interpreted as a symbol of what philosophical principle?
options: The pure consciousness or self (atman/jiva) held by the Lord
The illusion that binds the gods
The mortal frailty of the devas
questions.explanation: Some Vaishnava commentators read the Kaustubha as symbolizing the pure consciousness or souls cherished by Vishnu.

questions.q: The Kaustubha belongs to the cluster of Vishnu's emblems; which of the following is his discus rather than a jewel or ornament?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is Vishnu's discus weapon, distinct from the Kaustubha gem and his other ornaments.
